DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Geary Wilson 925-270-6017 Petitioner in Pro Per.
Geoffrey Mires, Esq. 510-465-3922 of Rankin Sproat Mires Reynolds Shuey & Mintz for Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides Argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The court grants defendants' request for judicial notice. (See People v. Mendoza (2015) 241 Cal.App.4th 764, 773, fn. 1.) Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action for declaratory and/or injunctive relief. (See CCP 430.10(e).) The allegations against the Alameda Court are time barred. The allegations against the Contra Costa and Solano courts do not allege an actual controversy or imminent harm. The case that plaintiff refers to is currently being litigated in San Francisco. Moreover, the court concludes that declaratory relief is not necessary and proper at this time. (See CCP 1061.) If a filing is rejected (even on an erroneous ground), plaintiff's remedy is to re-file the document and not file a lawsuit.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Newton Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/NL)
